roles.65364864: triggering alert: createdump.exe (.NET diagnostic LOLBin) invoked with target PID 632 and output filename dotnet-lsass.dmp — a possible LSASS dump
roles.65365536: repeat: createdump.exe -f dotnet-lsass.dmp with NO target PID specified
roles.65397981: repeat: createdump.exe -f dotnet-lsass.dmp, no target PID
roles.65399564: repeat: createdump.exe -f dotnet-lsass.dmp, no target PID
roles.65436350: repeat: createdump.exe -f dotnet-lsass.dmp, no target PID

metadata.security_proposition: createdump.exe targeted LSASS and produced a credential-access dump.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = WEAK. createdump.exe (a .NET LOLBin able to dump lsass) ran at High integrity with an lsass-SUGGESTING output filename (dotnet-lsass.dmp) and a target PID 632 — but target identity is UNVERIFIED (no process_create for 632, no lsass-by-name access, no dump file), and 4 of 5 invocations had no target PID. Calibration role = over-triage-prone (down-rank): a filename and a plausible PID are SUGGESTIVE, not proof; a model must not conclude 'LSASS was dumped'. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: createdump_windows-sysmon.log is host win-host-mhaag-attack-range-622; every other T1003.001/atomic_red_team log is a different host. Tier-1 single-sensor, verified.

boundary_definition: A0 = the createdump invocation WITH target PID 632 (12:43:15) — the only one specifying a target.
EV = the four repeated createdump invocations (12:43-13:02) with the same lsass-named output but NO target PID.
EID1 (process_create) ONLY — no EID10 lsass access and no EID11 dump file, so neither the target identity nor a successful dump is corroborated.
The output filename 'dotnet-lsass.dmp' is operator-chosen and is NOT proof the target is lsass.

main_alert_selection.selection_method: createdump.exe (a legitimate .NET process-dump utility) run at High integrity with an LSASS-suggesting output is the credential-access detection surface.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: The PID-632 invocation is the only one with a target; the output name and PID are suggestive but unverified. The package does not verify a digital signature.

event_groups.description: repeated createdump runs with the lsass-named output but no target PID.

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: WEAK / over-triage-prone. Suggestive (LOLBin + lsass-named output + plausible PID) but UNVERIFIED (no lsass-by-name access, no dump file, target-PID identity unconfirmed, 4/5 runs had no target). The model must not jump to 'lsass dumped -> High'.
This is the case that motivates the LSASS rubric TARGET-IDENTITY rule: an output filename and an unverified target PID are NOT proof of LSASS identity (the DECISIONS.md v0.1 known gap).
EID1-only capture; the createdump command lines are the evidence. No EID10/EID11 to corroborate the target or a successful dump.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
